Would it be possible to also specify those dependencies in the debian build?

Patch Set 5:

Sebastien Buisson, I have poor knowledge about Debian build process as uses it just few times - long time ago..

Patch Set 5:

Sebastian, I'm glad you pointed that out.  It seems wrong to fix deps for rpm but not .deb.  I see the control files under debian/.

Is it correct that the same additions should be made to control and control.main?
Do you know if the debian equivalent of krb5-devel is libkrb5-dev?  And equivalent of openssl-devel is libssl-dev?

Patch Set 6:

The debian packages we should require are, if I understand correctly:
- libkrb5-dev and libssl-dev for build dependencies;
- libgssapi-krb5-2 and libkrb5-3, libssl1.1 for binaries install.

The thing is I did not find any equivalent of %if in spec file for debian build. So I implemented a solution to change debian/control* files on the fly, depending on whether we are building with GSS support or not.

Please fee free to update or revert if this change is not appropriate.

Build fails on sles12sp4 x86_64 because it lacks openmpi2-devel:
   error: Failed build dependencies:
   	openmpi2-devel is needed by lustre-client-2.13.50_26_g22d05e5-1.x86_64

I have opened Jira ticket ATM-1636 to ask for this package to be installed.

Patch Set 8:

Sebastien - it looks patchiest 7 have an incorrect change.
I don't expect to make any changes in autoMakefile.am with this patch and it was never do in my local directory.
[root@lustre patches-LU-12214]# diffstat gss
 lustre.spec.in |    6 ++++--
 1 file changed, 4 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

Patch Set 8:

Could you please explain what is the incorrect change you are referring to? Build issues were due to patch #36427.

autoMakefile.am changes were introduced in order for this patch to also fix gss build on Debian. We should not rely on yet another patch to address gss build on Debian.

 > Sebastien - it looks patchiest 7 have an incorrect change.
 > I don't expect to make any changes in autoMakefile.am with this
 > patch and it was never do in my local directory.
 > [root@lustre patches-LU-12214]# diffstat gss

Patch Set 18:

This patch caused the following failure when install even we have libssl1.1+ installed

lustre-client-utils : Depends: libssl1.1+ but it is not installable

Patch Set 18:

> This patch caused the following failure when install even we have
 > libssl1.1+ installed
 > 
 > lustre-client-utils : Depends: libssl1.1+ but it is not installable

The exact name is not libssl1.1+, but libssl1.1.
I pushed a patch to fix this typo:
https://review.whamcloud.com/39167

commit.subject: LU-12214 build: fix build with gss enabled
commit.message:
LU-12214 build: fix build with gss enabled

provide a right dependences for the gss enabled lustre

Cray-bug-id: LUS-6033, LUS-7204
Test-parameters: trivial
Change-Id: Ib530a112f7f1629f7aea35cd4bad7c3f89e781ff
Signed-off-by: Alexey Lyashkov <c17817@cray.com>
Signed-off-by: Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>

Reviewed-by: Alexander Boyko <alexander.boyko@hpe.com>
